findings.evidence: `rg -n "mapValue_pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply|pointsMulEquiv_mapValue" TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup` shows inverse naturality lemmas for `MultiplicativeGroup` and `AdditiveGroup`, while `SplitTorus.lean` only has `pointsMulEquiv_mapValue` at lines 100-106.
findings.fix: Add a `@[simp]` theorem `AlgHom.mapValue φ ((pointsMulEquiv (A := A)).symm c) = (pointsMulEquiv (A := B)).symm (fun i => Units.map φ.toMonoidHom (c i))`, parallel to `MultiplicativeGroup.mapValue_pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply`.
findings.issue: `pointsMulEquiv_mapValue` gives naturality only after applying `pointsMulEquiv`; there is no public `mapValue_pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply` lemma for post-composing a point constructed from `c : σ → Aˣ`, so users must prove this by injectivity each time.

summary: The generator and arbitrary-character formulas are now present, but the split-torus API is still missing the naturality introduction rule for points built from coordinates.
